title: Split model routing — answer model varies, JSON phases stay fixed
description: Invariant 3: the answer/synthesis model is the user's pick (any provider — Berget, Anthropic claude-*, OpenAI gpt-*), while triage/gap/validation always run on the fixed reliable Berget JSON model. The batch picks a secondary-provider answer model and confirms the pipeline still completes with correct split billing.

points.label: Anthropic answer model completes the pipeline
points.summary: Pick a claude-* model, keep search ON, and send a research ask. PASS = a synthesized cited answer AND the research plan/gap steps still ran (those are Berget-Mistral under the hood). FAIL = an errored pipeline or JSON-phase failure when the answer model is non-Berget. (Skip if no claude-* model is in the dropdown — the provider key isn't set.)

actions.text: What are the leading approaches to grid-scale energy storage right now?

points.label: Provider switch mid-conversation stays coherent
points.summary: After the first answer, switch to a different provider's model and ask a follow-up. PASS = the follow-up answers coherently on the new model with history intact; FAIL = lost context or a routing error on the switch.

actions.text: Send the first message, then change the model in the dropdown and send the follow-up below.
Now compare those approaches on cost per kWh.
